arts.ttl: How to watch Girona vs Arsenal in Champions League: TV channel, live stream, permutations
arts.sum: The Champions League's newly refurbished league phase concludes on Wednesday, and Arsenal travel to Girona hoping to at least secure a top-eight finish and qualify for the round of 16

arts.ttl: Arsenal handed major Sverre Nypan transfer update as Evan Ferguson pathway emerges - Kieran King
arts.sum: football.london says : Although signing a midfielder is not Arsenal's priority, getting in Nypan under the noses of some of their rivals, including Villa and Tottenham, would be an astute piece of business. But if Arsenal are going to sign just one more player before the end of the window, a striker should be the one - and not another midfielder.

arts.ttl: Girona vs Arsenal: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Girona vs Arsenal Champions League preview, including team news, predicted lineups, how to watch on TV and more.

arts.ttl: Kieran Tierney to Celtic: Brendan Rodgers confirms when Arsenal defender will re-join Scottish giants
arts.sum: Brendan Rodgers confirms that Kieran Tierney's return to Celtic "will definitely take place in the summer", although there is still hope that the Arsenal defender could move to Glasgow this month.

arts.ttl: Arsenal: Mikel Arteta reveals Myles Lewis-Skelly reaction to Arsenal red card being overturned
arts.sum: Arteta was told about the successful appeal as Arsenal flew out to Spain

arts.ttl: Arsenal given reason to block Evan Ferguson January transfer from Brighton
arts.sum: Ferguson was once a 14-year-old debutant for Bohemians in a pre-season friendly against Chelsea, he was chased by Liverpool before Brighton landed his breakout signing, then he hit the Premier League with force. Brighton held out long enough to tie their talent down to a six-year deal in November 2023, and with it all hope gone of what was then touted to be £54m, based on Transfermarkt's valuation as of the month prior.

arts.ttl: Howard Webb sent strong message after Arsenal win Myles Lewis-Skelly red card appeal
arts.sum: Given the FA's decision to rescind Arsenal full-back Myles Lewis-Skelly's red card against Wolverhampton Wanderers, former PGMOL chief Keith Hackett expects current chief Howard Webb to reflect upon this latest mistake. Lewis-Skelly had been dismissed by Michael Oliver with a straight red card in the 43rd minute of Saturday's Premier League win at Molineux for tripping Matt Doherty on the edge of the Gunners ' penalty area.

arts.ttl: 'I always say to him': William Saliba says Arsenal have a £23m player who is truly 'incredible'
‘Let’s get this hatred out of football’: Mikel Arteta on threats against referee
arts.sum: Arsenal manager demands change in game’s ‘culture’ He wants new rules to stop abuse of players or officials Mikel Arteta has urged football to prioritise changing the culture that surrounds the game and eradicate “hatred”, saying: “We don’t want it, we don’t need it, it can only damage our sport, so let’s get it out.” Arteta’s plea was made after police opened an investigation regarding the threats directed at the referee Michael Oliver, whose home was placed under police guard after he sent off Myles Lewis-Skelly for a tackle on Matt Doherty at Wolves on Saturday. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arteta can unleash Kai Havertz at Arsenal with £80m Duran transfer
arts.sum: Arsenal signing Jhon Duran from Aston Villa in the January transfer window makes complete sense for three reasons. The arrival of Duran would not only provide Havertz with a needed rest, but it would also allow Arteta to use him in midfield again if he so wishes.
